heading: United States Navy Band; officer in charge
label: 8286 United States Navy Band; officer in charge
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6221(less 3d proviso).34 U.S.C. 596(less 3d proviso), .Mar. 4, 1925, ch. 536, § 1743 Stat. 1275Subsection (a) is set forth to preserve the legal authority for the title of the band, but the first 30 words of the , , are omitted as executed. To conform to the Career Compensation Act of 1949 ( et seq.), the word “basic” is inserted before “pay” in subsection (b) and the words “and is entitled to be credited for pay purposes with all service which may be credited under section 202 of the Career Compensation Act of 1949 ()” are substituted for the words , That all service as an enlisted man in the naval service shall be counted in computing longevity increases for pay of this leader”.Act of March 4, 1925, ch. 536, § 1743 Stat. 127537 U.S.C. 23137 U.S.C. 233“ProvidedThe second proviso is omitted as executed.

children.label: a
children.text: There is a Navy band known as the United States Navy Band.

An officer of the Navy designated for limited duty under section 8139 or 8146 of this title who is serving in a grade above lieutenant may be detailed by the Secretary of the Navy as Officer in Charge of the United States Navy Band.

While serving as Officer in Charge of the United States Navy Band, an officer shall hold the grade of captain if appointed to that grade by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. Such an appointment may be made notwithstanding .section 8146(d) of this title
